cards.grammarNote: Many French emotional states use avoir + noun rather than être + adjective. j'ai peur (not: je suis peureux). These are fixed expressions.
avoir peur = to be afraid (lit: to have fear). avoir peur de + noun/infinitive: j'ai peur des araignées = I'm afraid of spiders.
avoir honte = to be ashamed (lit: to have shame). avoir honte de: j'ai honte de ce que j'ai dit = I'm ashamed of what I said.
avoir envie de + infinitive = to feel like doing. J'ai envie de pleurer = I feel like crying. de rire = of laughing.

cards.back: J'ai du mal à me concentrer.
cards.grammarNote: avoir du mal à + infinitive = to have trouble doing. du mal = difficulty. à + infinitive follows.
avoir l'air + adjective = to seem/look. tu as l'air triste, il a l'air fatigué. The adjective agrees with the subject.
avoir le moral = to be in good spirits. ne pas avoir le moral = to be feeling down. Very common expression.
avoir besoin de + noun/infinitive = to need. de toi = of you (stressed pronoun after preposition).
avoir de la peine = to be in pain (emotionally)/to grieve. de la is the partitive article: la peine = sorrow.
avoir confiance en = to trust (lit: to have confidence in). en toi = in you. très common expression.
avoir le cœur brisé = to have a broken heart. brisé agrees with cœur (masculine): brisé, not brisée.

cards.front: I can't wait! / I'm excited about it.
cards.grammarNote: avoir hâte = to be eager/can't wait. J'ai hâte de te voir = I can't wait to see you. Very positive expression.
